title: Spanish Valenciana Paella with Grilled Nectarines
description: A classic Spanish dish from the Valencia region, paired with the sweetness of grilled nectarines. This paella recipe serves a crowd and is perfect for a summer evening.

ingredients.name: Valenciana Sausage
ingredients.notes: Spanish chorizo or morcilla can be substituted

ingredients.name: Uncured Chicken Breast
ingredients.notes: White meat is used to keep the dish lean

ingredients.name: Spanish Rice
ingredients.notes: Calasparra rice or other short-grain rice is best

ingredients.name: Artichoke Hearts
ingredients.notes: Canned or fresh artichokes can be used

ingredients.name: Roasted Red Peppers
ingredients.notes: Fresh peppers can be roasted in the oven

ingredients.name: Grilled Nectarines
ingredients.notes: Grilled to perfection and served on top

ingredients.name: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Fresh or canned tomatoes can be used

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality extra-virgin olive oil

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: A key ingredient in traditional paella

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced Valenciana sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on both sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the artichoke hearts, roasted red peppers, and diced tomato.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Add the Spanish rice to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the rice is lightly toasted.
Add 2 cups of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is cooked.
While the paella is cooking, grill the nectarines slices until caramelized.
Serve the paella hot, topped with grilled nectarines and fresh parsley.
